title: The Competent Authority pathway
metaTitle: Competent Authority pathway: skip the AMC exams (UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, NZ)
metaDescription: The Competent Authority pathway lets doctors certified in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada or New Zealand register in Australia without sitting the AMC exams, after a period of supervised practice.
summaryMarkdown: The Competent Authority pathway is for doctors — including general practitioners — who have been assessed and certified by a medical authority Australia recognises as a *competent authority*, and who have practised in that country. [[cite:ca-who]] Its big advantage: you generally **don't sit the AMC examinations**. [[cite:ca-noexams]] Instead you complete a period of supervised practice before general registration.

sections.heading: Recognised competent authorities
sections.bodyMarkdown:
Australia recognises a defined set of overseas bodies as competent authorities. These are generally: [[cite:ca-list]]

- **United Kingdom** — General Medical Council (GMC)
- **Ireland** — Medical Council of Ireland
- **United States** — ECFMG / USMLE
- **Canada** — Medical Council of Canada (LMCC)
- **New Zealand** — Medical Council of New Zealand (NZREX)

It isn't enough simply to have a degree from one of these countries — you must have been **assessed/certified by that authority and practised there**. [[cite:ca-who]] Always confirm your specific certificate qualifies.

sections.heading: Why you skip the AMC exams
sections.bodyMarkdown: Australia treats these training and certification systems as sufficiently comparable, so eligible applicants are not required to sit the AMC CAT MCQ or clinical assessment. [[cite:ca-noexams]] This is the main reason the pathway is faster than the [Standard pathway](/pathways/standard).

sections.heading: Supervised practice and registration
sections.bodyMarkdown: Eligible applicants apply for **provisional registration** and complete **12 months of supervised practice** before applying for general registration. [[cite:ca-supervised]] You'll need an approved position with a supervisor to do this. Registration types are explained on the [registration](/registration) page.

sections.heading: Other requirements
sections.bodyMarkdown: You still need to meet the standard registration requirements, including the English language standard, recency of practice and professional indemnity insurance. [[cite:ca-other]] See [English requirements](/english). Most roles are employer-sponsored (e.g. the Skills in Demand subclass 482 visa) — see [visas](/visas).

claims.text: The pathway is for IMGs (including GPs and specialists) assessed by a competent authority who have practised in a competent authority country.
IMGs on the competent authority pathway generally do not sit the AMC examinations.
Recognised competent authorities include the GMC (UK), Medical Council of Ireland, ECFMG/USMLE (USA), Medical Council of Canada (LMCC) and Medical Council of New Zealand (NZREX).
Eligible applicants take provisional registration and complete 12 months of supervised practice before general registration.
Applicants must still meet registration standards including English language skills, recency of practice and indemnity insurance.

faq.question: I have a UK degree but trained as a junior doctor elsewhere — do I qualify?
faq.answerMarkdown: Eligibility depends on being certified by the competent authority **and** having practised in that country, not just holding the degree. Check your specific situation with the Medical Board.

faq.question: Do GPs use this pathway?
faq.answerMarkdown: Yes — the competent authority pathway is open to general practitioners as well as other non-specialists and specialists assessed by a competent authority.

faq.question: Is supervised practice the same as an internship?
faq.answerMarkdown: It's a supervised period (commonly 12 months) in an approved position, after which you can apply for general registration.
